channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Grizzlies' Ja Morant's home burglarized as part of Chilean crime ring: reports
item.description: The Chilean migrants who robbed the homes of Travis Kelce, Patrick Mahomes, and Joe Burrow did the same to Ja Morant's house, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ESPN and Major League Baseball will end their national television deal after 2025 season
item.description: ESPN’s coverage of Major League Baseball games — at least in its current form — will conclude at the end of the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Joe Reedy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Zack Martin is retiring after a decorated career at guard for the Cowboys, AP sources say
item.description: Zack Martin is retiring after a decorated career that included seven All-Pro seasons at right guard with the Dallas Cowboys, two people with knowledge of the decision said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dodgers pitcher Bobby Miller hit in the head by a line drive, walks off field under own power
item.description: Los Angeles Dodgers pitcher Bobby Miller was hit in the head by a line drive during the team’s spring training against the Chicago Cubs, but was able to walk off the field.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nationals' Trevor Williams recalls fighting anti-Catholic mockery when Dodgers hosted drag nun group
item.description: Washington Nationals pitcher Trevor Williams talked about fighting back against anti-Catholic mockery during the 2023 season when one team hosted the Sisterhood of Perpetual Indulgence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NBA suspends Bucks player Bobby Portis Jr. for drug policy violation
item.description: The suspension is happening just when the Bucks appeared on the verge of getting their full roster together after making multiple moves at the trade deadline, including the trade of three-time All-Star wing Khris Middleton to the Washington Wizards.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Robot umpires are getting their first MLB test during spring training
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — A computerized system that calls balls and strikes is being tested during Major League Baseball spring training exhibition games starting Thursday after four years of experiments in the minor leagues.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: American fans lightly boo `O Canada' before 4 Nations Face-Off title game
item.description: American fans lightly booed the Canadian national anthem and roared out their own Thursday night before the championship game of the 4 Nations Face-Off, a matchup between the United States and Canada that has taken on collateral importance in the wake of cross-border political animosity.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tiger Woods joins another White House meeting as PGA Tour moves closer to Saudi deal
item.description: Tiger Woods and PGA Tour Commissioner Jay Monahan have taken part of a White House meeting with President Donald Trump that signals more progress in getting a deal done with the Saudi backers of LIV Golf.
item.dc:creator: Doug Ferguson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Missouri coach Dennis Gates takes PA microphone to head off court-storming celebration
item.description: COLUMBIA, Mo. (AP) — After Missouri basketball fans stormed the court following a victory over then-No. 1 Kansas on Dec. 8, Tigers coach Dennis Gates made sure there wouldn't be an encore after No. 15 Missouri beat No. 4 Alabama 110-98 on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: DAVID SOLOMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspects charged in connection to burglaries of several pro athletes
item.description: Seven Chilean nationals face federal charges related to the burglaries of several high profile athletes' homes.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, WGN 9

item.title: Reports: Cubs sign veteran 3B Justin Turner
item.description: CHICAGO — The Cubs are signing veteran free agent third baseman Justin Turner, according to multiple reports. Patrick Mooney with The Athletic reports that the deal is for $6 million for one year.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: There is a ‘renaissance’ in San Francisco, NBA commissioner says
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- For the past few days, San Francisco was the center of the basketball universe. It was the first time in 25 years NBA All-Star weekend was in the Bay Area.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Northwestern golfers invited to Augusta National Women's Amateur
item.description: EVANSTON, Ill. - Northwestern is going to be well-represented at the 6th Annual Augusta National Women's Amateur Championship. Lauryn Nguyen and Elise Lee are set to the make the trip to Georgia along with NU commit Arianna Lau. "It's such a special place," noted Nguyen.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sharkey, WGN 9

item.title: Angels star Mike Trout moving from center to right field in hopes of staying more healthy
item.description: TEMPE, Ariz. (AP) — Los Angeles Angels oft-injured star Mike Trout is moving from center to right field in hopes of better preserving his health. The three-time AL MVP played in 82 or fewer games in three of the past four seasons while dealing with an assortment of injuries.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Shaq's OGs win the 1st NBA All-Star mini-tournament, topping Chuck's Global Stars 41-25 in the final
item.description: An All-Star Game like none other — since it was three games, not one — still had plenty of flash and not a lot of fire. And in the end, Boston's Jayson Tatum had the dunk that decided a crown.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Seven more massage therapists accuse Ravens' Tucker of sexually inappropriate conduct: report
item.description: A total of 16 massage therapists from eight Baltimore-area spas have now shared accounts with the Baltimore Banner.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Michigan State coach Tom Izzo passes Bob Knight for most Big Ten conference wins
item.description: CHAMPAIGN, Ill. (AP) — Michigan State’s Tom Izzo followed one of the most disappointing losses of his coaching career with a record-setting victory. For Illinois, it was a game in which nothing went right after halftime — or during it, thanks to an unfortunate jersey-raising snafu. The No.
item.dc:creator: STEVE STEIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Final field of 41 set for Daytona 500; Bubba Wallace, Austin Cindric win Duels
item.description: This Sunday at 1:30 p.m., the NASCAR series kicks off with the legendary Daytona 500 at Daytona International Speedway.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, NewsNation

item.title: Avid volunteer enjoys first Genesis Invitational
item.description: Jenny Liu has volunteered at Torrey Pines for 24 years. She’s seen a lot in her time, but this week it was her first experience with the Genesis Invitational.
item.dc:creator: Frank Jefferson, Fox 5

item.title: Jazz fall to Clippers in final game before All-Star break
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Norman Powell scored a season-high 41 points and James Harden added 32 to lift the Los Angeles Clippers to a 120-116 overtime victory over the Utah Jazz on Thursday night. Harden also had 10 rebounds, seven assists and three steals.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
